steps.text: Assess the stack gas moisture levels and check for the presence of acid gases.
Choose the appropriate sampling system setup based on moisture and acid gas findings.
Thoroughly clean all glassware, coated stainless-steel fittings, and heated sample lines.
Set up the impinger conditioning system if moisture or acid gas management is required.
Assemble the sampling train by connecting the probe, filter, transfer line, critical orifice, and bypass pump.
Apply heat to the sampling train components once assembly is complete.
Turn on and run the bypass pump at a flow rate of 1 to 2 Lpm.
Perform a system leak check to ensure there are no leaks.
Verify the initial vacuum of the canister using a vacuum gauge.
Connect the canister to the critical orifice on the sampling train.
Place the probe and filter assembly into the duct at the first traverse point.
Run the bypass pump to purge the sampling line for at least two minutes.
Open the canister valve, note the start time, and begin sample collection.
Move the probe to sample from at least three discrete traverse points for equal durations.
Close the canister valve, record the stop time, perform a post-test leak check, and disconnect the canister.
Measure and record the final vacuum of the canister.
Close the canister valve, detach the gauge, and secure the inlet cap.
Fill out the chain of custody documentation completely.
Pack the canisters into protective containers and shipping boxes.
Store the canisters at ambient laboratory temperature and ship them for analysis.
Measure and document the canister pressure upon arrival at the laboratory.
Perform the laboratory analysis of the samples within 30 days of collection.

preconditions.text: The Direct VFC Sampling System is selected if stack moisture is 3% or less and acid gases are absent.
The Water/Acid Gas Management system is selected if stack moisture exceeds 3% or acid gases are present.
A vacuum gauge must be attached and the valve opened before connecting the canister to the train.
The sampling train must be fully assembled, heated, and stabilized at the target temperature before starting collection.

constraints.text: Use extreme caution when handling high-pressure gas cylinders.
Avoid exposure to suspected carcinogens and hazardous compounds by following safety data sheets.
The bypass pump flow rate must be maintained between 1 and 2 Lpm.
Do not open the canister valve if the leak check fails.
